---
title: "KNN"
author: "Aditya Kataria"
date: "2026-04-18"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
student <- read.csv("./student/student-por.csv", sep = ";")
```


```{r}
library(class)
set.seed(321)

# same predictors as AIC model 
predictors <- c("school", "failures", "higher", "sex", "absences",
                "guardian", "paid", "Fedu")

df_knn <- student[, c(predictors, "G3")]

# Encode factors as numeric
df_knn$school   <- ifelse(df_knn$school == "MS", 1, 0)
df_knn$higher   <- ifelse(df_knn$higher == "yes", 1, 0)
df_knn$sex      <- ifelse(df_knn$sex == "M", 1, 0)
df_knn$paid     <- ifelse(df_knn$paid == "yes", 1, 0)
df_knn$guardian <- ifelse(df_knn$guardian == "mother", 1,
                   ifelse(df_knn$guardian == "other", 2, 0))  # father=0, mother=1, other=2
df_knn$G3 <- ifelse(df_knn$G3 >= 12, 1, 0)
df_knn <- na.omit(df_knn)

nrow(df_knn)

# Train/test split (70/30)
set.seed(1)
train_idx <- sample(1:nrow(df_knn), 0.7 * nrow(df_knn))
train <- df_knn[train_idx, ]
test  <- df_knn[-train_idx, ]

train_scaled <- scale(train[, predictors])
train_center <- attr(train_scaled, "scaled:center")
train_scale  <- attr(train_scaled, "scaled:scale")

train_X <- as.matrix(train_scaled)
test_X  <- as.matrix(scale(test[, predictors],
                            center = train_center,
                            scale  = train_scale))
train_Y <- train$G3
test_Y  <- test$G3

# Find optimal K
K <- 1:50
err_vals <- numeric(50)

for (k in K) {
  pred <- knn(train_X, test_X, train_Y, k = k)
  err_vals[k] <- mean(pred != test_Y)
}

best_k <- which.min(err_vals)
best_k
1 - err_vals[best_k]

plot(K, err_vals, type = "b", xlab = "K", ylab = "Test Error Rate",
     main = "KNN Classification - Test Error Rate vs K")
```
The best K is 25 with a test accuracy of 74.9%. This represents an improvement over logistic regression's 73.3% accuracy, suggesting that the structure of KNN better captures the relationships between these predictors and student pass/fail outcomes.
